perturbation.original_text: The confidentiality obligations under this Agreement shall re main in effect for the longer of (i) five (5) years after the termination of this Agreement, or (ii) five (5) years after the expiration or termina tion of th e Business Purpose. Notwithstanding the foregoing, with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a Trade Secret, the confidentiality obligations under this Agreement shall survive as long as the information disclosed continues to constitute a T rade Secret under applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidentiality obligations under this Agreement shall remain in effect for the shorter of (i) five (5) years after the termination of this Agreement, or (ii) five (5) years after the expiration or termina tion of th e Business Purpose. Notwithstanding the foregoing, with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a Trade Secret, the confidentiality obligations under this Agreement shall terminate after one (1) year, regardless if the information disclosed continues to constitute a T rade Secret under applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "longer" to "shorter" creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, it implies that the confidentiality obligations are extended based on which timeframe is longer. By switching to "shorter", the confidentiality obligations are reduced. This contradicts the subsequent clause about Trade Secrets, which originally ensured indefinite protection. The alteration makes it unclear how long trade secrets are protected, because the general time period is shorter.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Continuing Obligation . The confidentiality obligations under this Agreement shall remain in effect for the shorter of (i) five (5) years after the termination of this Agreement, or (ii) five (5) years after the expiration or termina tion of th e Business Purpose. Notwithstanding the foregoing, with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a Trade Secret, the confidentiality obligations under this Agreement shall terminate after one (1) year, regardless if the information disclosed continues to constitute a T rade Secret under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'longer' to 'shorter' and altering the trade secret protection from indefinite to one year introduces a contradiction. The changed text in the first paragraph limits the confidentiality obligations to a shorter timeframe, conflicting with the original intent of the trade secret protection. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text. The changed text significantly reduces the duration of confidentiality, creating a clear conflict. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party hereby agrees that any Confidential Information received by it will be used solely for the Business Purpose, and that such information will be kept confidential by such Party; provided, however, that such Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Representatives of the Receiving Party on a need to know basis to the extent necessary to further the Business Purpose (it being understood that such Representatives shall be informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed to treat such information confidentially and that the Rec eiving Party shall take all reasonable measures to restrain
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party hereby agrees that any Confidential Information received by it will be used solely for the Business Purpose, but such information does not need to be kept confidential by such Party; provided, however, that such Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Representatives of the Receiving Party on a need to know basis to the extent necessary to further the Business Purpose (it being understood that such Representatives shall be informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed to treat such information confidentially and that the Rec eiving Party shall take all reasonable measures to restrain
perturbation.explanation: By stating that confidential information received does not need to be kept confidential, a contradiction is introduced. This directly conflicts with the later part of the same sentence that specifies how Representatives should handle the information confidentially. The contradiction creates uncertainty about the core obligation of confidentiality, initially said it had to be kept confidential, but a perturbation was made to create an in-text contradiction. This leads to it being uncertain whether the party needs to keep it confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information . Each Party hereby agrees that any Confidential Information received by it will be used solely for the Business Purpose, but such information does not need to be kept confidential by such Party; provided, however, that such Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Representatives of the Receiving Party on a need to know basis to the extent necessary to further the Business Purpose (it being understood that such Representatives shall be informed by the Receiving Party of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed to treat such information confidentially and that the Rec eiving Party shall take all reasonable measures to restrain
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that confidential information received does not need to be kept confidential, while the original text states that it will be kept confidential. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text. This creates a direct and obvious conflict within the document regarding the fundamental obligation of confidentiality. The phrase 'but such information does not need to be kept confidential' directly contradicts the rest of the section, creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) calendar days of written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return to the Disclosing Party, or at the Disclosing Party's option, shall destroy, any physical and written records containing such Confidential Information, whether such physical and written records were sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party or were prepared by the R ecipient or its Representatives from information sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party. In such event, the R ecipient shall certify in writing to the Disclosing P arty that such physical or written materials have been returned o r destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Within ten (10) calendar days of written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party is NOT required to return to the Disclosing Party, nor is the Receiving Party required to destroy, any physical and written records containing such Confidential Information, whether such physical and written records were sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party or were prepared by the R ecipient or its Representatives from information sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party. In such event, the R ecipient shall certify in writing to the Disclosing P arty that such physical or written materials have been returned o r destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the requirement to return or destroy confidential information to explicitly stating that the receiving party is not required to do so, it directly contradicts the later sentence that expects certification of return or destruction. If the Receiving Party is not required to return or destroy the information, then they can not certify that those records have been returned or destroyed. This create an in-text contradiction. The initial statement removes the obligation, while the subsequent statement assumes that it happened.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return . Within ten (10) calendar days of written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party is NOT required to return to the Disclosing Party, nor is the Receiving Party required to destroy, any physical and written records containing such Confidential Information, whether such physical and written records were sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party or were prepared by the R ecipient or its Representatives from information sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party. In such event, the R ecipient shall certify in writing to the Disclosing P arty that such physical or written materials have been returned o r destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states the Receiving Party is NOT required to return or destroy confidential information. However, the contradicted text states that 'the R ecipient shall certify in writing to the Disclosing P arty that such physical or written materials have been returned o r destroyed.' This creates a direct contradiction because the Receiving Party cannot certify something they are not required to do. Therefore, the change introduces a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party he reby agrees that a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, and all copies and manifestations of the same, or in any way incorporating or reflecting same, including but not limited to all notes, data, reference materials, sketches, drawings, memoranda, documentation, computer files and disks incorporating or reflecting any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, are, and shall remain at all times, the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and that no right, license title license or inte rest in the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information is granted by this Agreement or by any disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party he reby agrees that a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, and all copies and manifestations of the same, or in any way incorporating or reflecting same, including but not limited to all notes, data, reference materials, sketches, drawings, memoranda, documentation, computer files and disks incorporating or reflecting any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, are not, and shall not remain at all times, the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party but of the recieving party, and that right, license title license or inte rest in the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information is granted by this Agreement or by any disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause clearly states that Confidential Information remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. The changed clause states that the confidential information is not the property of the disclosing party but of the receiving party, but then grants a right, license, title, and interest to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information through the agreement. This internal contradiction blurs the ownership rights, making it uncertain which party controls the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership. Each Party he reby agrees that a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, and all copies and manifestations of the same, or in any way incorporating or reflecting same, including but not limited to all notes, data, reference materials, sketches, drawings, memoranda, documentation, computer files and disks incorporating or reflecting any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, are not, and shall not remain at all times, the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party but of the recieving party, and that right, license title license or inte rest in the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information is granted by this Agreement or by any disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and the contradicted text. It first states the Confidential Information is *not* the property of the Disclosing Party but of the Receiving Party. Then, it says that a right, license, title, and interest in the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information *is granted* by the agreement. This creates an internal contradiction regarding who owns and controls the Confidential Information, which aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and its rights and obligations shall not be assignable without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, and any attempted assignment without such consent shall be void; provided , however , that such consent shall not be require d in the case of an assignment to a successor to the business and assets of the assignor, which successor agrees to be bound to the terms of this Agreement and the obligations of the assignor under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and its rights and obligations shall not be assignable without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, and any attempted assignment without such consent shall be valid; provided , however , that such consent shall not be require d in the case of an assignment to a successor to the business and assets of the assignor, which successor refuses to be bound to the terms of this Agreement and the obligations of the assignor under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that an unconsented assignment is void, meaning it has no legal effect. Furthermore, successors must agree to be bound by the terms. The modified text contradicts this, stating an unconsented assignment is valid and a successor is free to refuse to the terms. This introduces uncertainty: is the agreement assignable without consent, and is a successor bound by the original terms? The clauses creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Successors and Assigns . This Agreement and its rights and obligations shall not be assignable without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, and any attempted assignment without such consent shall be valid; provided , however , that such consent shall not be require d in the case of an assignment to a successor to the business and assets of the assignor, which successor refuses to be bound to the terms of this Agreement and the obligations of the assignor under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by stating that an unconsented assignment is valid and that a successor is free to refuse the terms of the agreement, in direct contradiction to the original text that an unconsented assignment is void and successors agree to be bound by the terms. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, which directly contradicts the original statement that unconsented assignments are void, and successors must agree to be bound.
